reasoning: The Lean hypothesis `∀ x y, |f x - f y| ≤ |x - y| ^ 2` directly matches the informal condition $|f(t)-f(x)| \leq |t-x|^2$ for all $t, x$ (just with variables renamed). The conclusion `∃ c, f = λ _x => c` asserts that $f$ is the constant function equal to some $c$, which is the standard way to express "$f$ is constant" and is logically equivalent to "$\forall x\, y,\, f(x) = f(y)$" since $\mathbb{R}$ is nonempty. Types ($\mathbb{R} \to \mathbb{R}$), quantifier structure, and the norm/absolute-value encoding all match. No hypotheses are added or dropped.
